title: Kuwait Finance House (KFH Egypt) (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Kuwait Finance House (KFH Egypt) in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Kuwait Finance House (KFH Egypt)?
faq.answer: Kuwait Finance House (KFH Egypt) is a fully Sharia-compliant Islamic bank operating in Egypt. It is part of the Kuwait Finance House Group, one of the world's largest Islamic financial institutions. KFH Egypt was established following KFH's acquisition of Ahli United Bank Bahrain in 2022 and its subsequent rebranding in January 2025. The bank operates as a universal Islamic banking institution, serving individuals, corporations, and organizations across Egypt. With 44 branches nationwide and a total asset base of EGP 144 billion as of 2024, KFH Egypt ranks among the significant Islamic banking players in the Egyptian market.

faq.question: When was Kuwait Finance House (KFH Egypt) founded?
faq.answer: KFH Egypt officially launched its operations in January 2025, following a strategic transformation. The bank's origins trace back to Ahli United Bank Egypt, which was acquired by Kuwait Finance House as part of its 2022 merger with Ahli United Bank Bahrain. The transformation into a fully Sharia-compliant institution was completed in December 2023. In February 2024, KFH merged with Ahli United Bank Kuwait, strengthening its regional presence. The rebranding to KFH Egypt was finalized in January 2025, marking the bank's complete integration into the KFH Group under the slogan "Horizons Without Limits."

faq.question: Where is Kuwait Finance House (KFH Egypt) headquartered?
faq.answer: KFH Egypt is headquartered in New Cairo, Egypt. The bank's main office is located at Plot 81, Banks Zone, 5th Settlement, New Cairo 11835. This location positions the bank within Egypt's modern banking district, providing access to corporate clients and financial institutions. The headquarters houses the bank's primary administrative and operational functions.

faq.question: Where does Kuwait Finance House (KFH Egypt) operate?
faq.answer: KFH Egypt operates throughout Egypt with a network of 44 branches distributed across major cities and regions. The branch network provides comprehensive coverage in Cairo, Alexandria, and other key urban centers. The bank focuses its operations on areas with significant commercial activity and population density. This nationwide presence enables KFH Egypt to serve both retail and corporate customers across Egypt's diverse geographic and economic landscape. The bank's digital banking infrastructure extends its reach beyond physical branches, allowing customers throughout Egypt to access banking services.

faq.question: What services does Kuwait Finance House (KFH Egypt) offer?
faq.answer: KFH Egypt provides a comprehensive range of Sharia-compliant banking products and services, including: Retail Banking: Current accounts, savings accounts, personal financing solutions, vehicle financing (Ijara), home financing, and credit cards with various benefits and rewards programs. Corporate Banking: Commercial financing, trade finance solutions, treasury services, and cash management products tailored to businesses and organizations. Investment Services: Investment portfolios, real estate financing, and wealth management solutions structured in accordance with Islamic principles. Digital Banking: KFH Egypt Mobile Banking app for iOS and Android devices, offering 24/7 account access, fund transfers within own accounts and to other KFH accounts, local bank transfers, credit card payments, cardless cash withdrawal, investment deposit opening, and branch/ATM locator services. The bank also provides online banking platforms, digital onboarding through the KFH Egypt Digital Onboarding app, and a digital wallet solution. Additional Services: Donation payments, currency conversion tools, e-statements, SMS banking services, and WhatsApp customer support at 01555819072.

faq.question: What makes Kuwait Finance House (KFH Egypt) unique?
faq.answer: KFH Egypt distinguishes itself as a newly established Islamic bank backed by one of the world's largest Islamic financial institutions. The parent KFH Group operates across 12 countries with over 680 branches and approximately $120 billion in assets. The bank's complete transformation from a conventional bank to a fully Sharia-compliant institution demonstrates its commitment to Islamic banking principles. All products and services are overseen by an independent Shariah Committee ensuring compliance with Islamic law. KFH Egypt's digital banking capabilities include comprehensive mobile and online platforms launched shortly after rebranding, providing modern banking convenience within a Sharia-compliant framework. The bank's digital onboarding solution allows customers to open accounts entirely through their smartphones. The institution reported exceptional growth in its first year, with net consolidated profits surging 65% to EGP 5.5 billion in 2024. Customer deposits grew 30% to EGP 117 billion, and the financing portfolio expanded 26% to EGP 85.3 billion.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Kuwait Finance House (KFH Egypt)?
faq.answer: KFH Egypt is an Islamic Bank operating under full Sharia compliance as supervised by Egypt's Central Bank. Islamic banks in Egypt operate according to principles of Islamic finance (Shari'a), which prohibit interest (riba) and speculation (gharar). Instead of charging interest, Islamic banks operate on profit-and-loss sharing models and asset-backed financing structures. All transactions must be based on tangible assets and real economic activity. KFH Egypt functions as a universal Islamic bank, offering retail, commercial, and investment banking services. The bank serves individuals, small and medium enterprises, corporations, and institutional clients, all within the framework of Islamic banking principles. The institution adheres to Islamic banking governance standards, including oversight by a Shariah Supervisory Board that ensures all products, services, and operations comply with Islamic law.

faq.question: What is KFH Egypt's relationship with the Kuwait Finance House Group?
faq.answer: KFH Egypt is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Kuwait Finance House (KFH), which was established in 1977 as the world's first Islamic bank. The parent company is listed on the Kuwait Stock Exchange and Bahrain Bourse. Kuwait Finance House ranks as the largest bank in Kuwait by market capitalization (approximately KWD 13 billion) and is the world's second-largest Islamic bank. The KFH Group has been a pioneer in Islamic banking for over 47 years. KFH Egypt benefits from the group's extensive experience, global best practices, financial strength, and technological infrastructure. The parent organization's expertise in Sharia-compliant banking provides KFH Egypt with a competitive advantage in the Egyptian Islamic banking market. The integration into the KFH Group gives the Egyptian operation access to international sukuk markets, cross-border Islamic finance solutions, and a global network spanning Kuwait, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Turkey, Malaysia, Germany, the United Kingdom, Iraq, Oman, and Libya.
